text:
The mother of Stoke-on-Trent's Olympic silver medallist Adam Burgess has told how he cried on her shoulder following the men's canoe singles final.

Stoke Cityfan Burgess, from Trentham, produced a sensational run down the white water to win his first Olympic medal by clocking a time of 96.84 seconds at the Paris Games. The 32-year-old had missed out on the podium by 0.16 seconds at the delayed Tokyo Games.

Mum Carole told BBC sport she saw him for only a couple of minutes after he received his medal and 'managed to give him a really big hug'. She added: "He was crying on my shoulder. I just said 'congratulations Adam, this is just absolutely awesome'.

READ:Stoke-on-Trent's Adam Burgess wins silver at Paris OlympicsAdam Burgess learned to canoe on the River Trent and now he's on the podium at the Olympics

READ MORE:Adam Peaty - The rise, fall and rise again of 'The Beast' as Olympic immortality awaits in Paris

"It's been 20-odd years coming, from that very first time I took him down to the river in Stone, all those days by the river bank... all the trips all round Britain for races as he was going through the ranks.

"It's been [an] absolutely incredible journey and I'm so happy, so proud that I've been with him the whole time."

Adam Burgess had said previously: "I really think [the] raw speed that I've got, that's what was forged in the early days back on the River Trent in Stone."

Brother Matthew, who flew over from Hong Kong for the event, said he had received hundreds of messages from friends sending him photos of the podium scenes.

He told the BBC: "I had to watch him in a bar in Hong Kong last time. Obviously Paris is a bit further away from Hong Kong where I live, but completely worth it. I've slightly jumped on the bandwagon. These guys [other family] have sort of followed him for 20 years and [are] a lot more emotionally invested than I am. But, yeah, I think I perhaps was crying the most there at the end."

It was formerRadio Stoke presenter John Acreswho was commentating on Radio 5 Live as Burgess (Stafford & Stone Canoe Club) secured the greatest moment of his career.

He said: "Adam can’t believe it. The look on his face, he thought he'd lost it in that middle section. That is an incredible bottom section for the young man from Stoke on Trent. He cant believe it, he’s punching his arms to the skies and he just shouted, 'Come on'.

"That’s all Adam Burgess has dreamed off, being on the podium. All that breath work he has done, the agony of Tokyo and being so close to a medal – that will be absolutely amazing for him and all his family. They said if they got on the podium, that will do us after Tokyo."

title: Stoke-on-Trent's Olympic medallist Adam Burgess cries on mum's shoulder - Stoke-on-Trent Live
author: Hayley Parker
description: Adam Burgess, from Trentham, clinched silver at Paris 2024
